title: Encouraging Manoah
summary: “And Manoah said unto his wife, We shall surely die, because we have seen God. But his wife said unto him, If the LORD were pleased to kill us, he would not have received a burnt offering and a meat offering at our hands, neither would he have shewed us all these things, nor would as at this time have told us such things as these.”(Judges 13:22-23). The marriage of Job and the marriage of Manoah were both founded on faith and righteousness. They loved God and were faithful in every way until both houses received supernatural visitation. It is a paradox that Christian marriages can be strong and stable until the supernatural intervenes. In the case of Job, it was Satan who intervened violently, while an angel of God visited the house of Manoah. In the case of Job’s house, his wife crumbled under the pressure. “Then said his wife unto him, Dost thou still retain thine integrity? curse God, and die” (Job 2:9). The roles were reversed in Manoah’s household – the angel’s visitation brought a mortal fear upon him and he was certain the two of them would die, but his wife countered with 3 powerful arguments. She was certain that God sent the angel to bless them, which we will consider more carefully later. May I ask us all to reflect on the above for a moment! We look to heaven and mention heaven often in our walk with the Lord Jesus Christ, but what will a visitation from heaven reveal about our faith and love of God? The Bible is full of mixed responses when angels appeared to the people of God: Jacob recognized the angel at Peniel as an opportunity and an invitation to change his character forever while Zachariah, the father of John the Baptist, was struck with muteness because of his unbelief. What will heaven reveal about your faith whether you are married or single?
